description: In C, when pkg-config entries, vendored headers, and the libraries listed in LDLIBS change, verify the lockfile, generated artifacts, and a runtime smoke.
body: In C, a dependency change ripples through pkg-config entries, vendored headers, and the libraries listed in LDLIBS, generated bindings, and runtime behavior, so it needs more than a recompile. Use when pkg-config entries, vendored headers, and the libraries listed in LDLIBS are added, removed, or version-bumped. Do not merge a dependency bump on a green compile alone, and avoid editing the lockfile by hand. Actions: inspect the dependency delta, re-resolve and review pkg-config entries, vendored headers, and the libraries listed in LDLIBS, regenerate affected outputs from lex/yacc, autoconf (configure.ac), or codegen scripts that emit .c/.h, then run the unit binary plus valgrind on the changed translation unit and smoke the import/runtime path. Validation signals include a reviewed lockfile diff, a clean clang-tidy, cppcheck, and -fanalyzer warnings pass, and a passing runtime smoke. Risk boundary: escalate on a major-version bump, a transitive conflict you cannot resolve, or a license/security flag.
